Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Irving McNeil Ives papers
Ives, Irving McNeil, 1896-1962.
1913
248 cubic feet. 248 cubic feet.
1862-1958,-1933-1958 (bulk) Correspondence, speeches, photographs, audio tapes, motion picture films, phonograph records, drawings, plans, clippings Ives's papers, which pertain chiefly to his service in the U.S. Senate, include correspondence, memoranda, and other material from constituents, fellow legislators, executive departments, and the general public concerning appointments, bills sponsored or co-sponsored by Ives, committee hearings, education, federal agencies, commissions, and departments, foreign affairs and foreign aid, labor and public welfare, international organizations, national defense and internal security, patronage, political campaigns, public utilities, private bills, Senate bills, social security, tariff and trade agreements, taxation, transportation, veterans' affairs, and numerous other subjects. Also included are records pertaining to his years as a New York State Assemblyman; his support of his Alma Mater, Hamilton College; family and personal papers; and materials relating to Ives's insurance and banking businesses. Collection material in English 2086127
General
Contact Information:

Julia ParkerPeter Martinez
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, September 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Politician.
Irving McNeil Ives was a member of the New York State Assembly, 1933-46; author and sponsor of legislation creating the New York State Department of Commerce and the New York State School of Industrial and Labor Relations at Cornell University; co-author and co-sponsor of a New York State anti-discrimination law; Dean of the Industrial and Labor Relations School at Cornell, 1945-47; United States senator, 1947-59; member of the Committee on Appropriations, the Committee on Labor and Public Welfare; vice-chairman of the Senate Select Committee on Improper Activities in the Labor and Management Field, 1957; President of the International Labor Organization Conference, Geneva, 1953; Republican candidate for governor of New York, 1954.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Ives's papers, which pertain chiefly to his service in the U.S. Senate, include correspondence, memoranda, and other material from constituents, fellow legislators, executive departments, and the general public concerning appointments, bills sponsored or co-sponsored by Ives, committee hearings, education, federal agencies, commissions, and departments, foreign affairs and foreign aid, labor and public welfare, international organizations, national defense and internal security, patronage, political campaigns, public utilities, private bills, Senate bills, social security, tariff and trade agreements, taxation, transportation, veterans' affairs, and numerous other subjects. Also included are records pertaining to his years as a New York State Assemblyman; his support of his Alma Mater, Hamilton College; family and personal papers; and materials relating to Ives's insurance and banking businesses.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence and Legislation Boxes 1-149, 152-158, 164, 170-181
Series II. Campaigns Boxes 49, 70, 166, 169, 173-174, 188-190, 225, 230, 237, 239-248
Series III. Speeches Boxes 150-151, 165-166, 182, 187-188, 205, 228, 231, 236-239, 242, 245-246, 248, 251
Series IV. Honors/Awards Boxes 165, 170, 221, 251
Series V. Hamilton College Boxes 5, 24, 55, 188, 246-247
Series VI. Memorabilia Boxes 167-170, 249, 251-252
Series VII. Photographs Boxes 159-163, 166, 171, 251
Series VIII. Audio Tapes Box 249
Series IX. Motion Picture Films Box 250
Series X. Phonograph Records Box 250
Series XI. Drawings/Plans Mapcase
Series XII. Clippings Boxes 137, 188, 217, 224, 231, 241-243
Series XIII. Miscellany Boxes 8, 29, 43, 50, 61, 267, 176, 188, 249, 251
Cite As:
Irving McNeil Ives papers, #1913. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
